name: Jacques Ibert
abstract: Jacques François Antoine Marie Ibert (15 August 1890 – 5 February 1962) was a French composer of classical music. Having studied music from an early age, he studied at the Paris Conservatoire and won its top prize, the Prix de Rome at his first attempt, despite studies interrupted by his service in World War I.

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Watts
about_quotes.evidence: I performed a very famous saxophone piece by Jacques Ibert called “Concertina da Camera,” played that with orchestra.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ernie Watts interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I was thinking of English composers, like Jacques Iber, and of course Debussy. I didn’t play too many of his things until later.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
